The AI playbook for people who run things.

Every Tuesday: five plays your team can actually run — drawn from owners and operators using agentic AI to do real work. No demos. No hype. Just what's working, what isn't, and how to start.

Real owners · Real builds

What's working right now

🏠
Issue #002 · Founder

Run your business on AI (while living your life)

Jesse Genet runs her company on agentic AI from a Mac Mini. Amazon orders, printing, procurement — all automated while she homeschools 4 kids.
💸
Issue #003 · Cost cutting

Replace your SaaS stack with one AI agent

One founder cut $500/month in SaaS fees to zero using a single AI agent on a Mac Mini. Here's how to replicate it.
🔧
Issue #007 · Trades

This HVAC guy spent Friday night setting up AI. Now his estimates write themselves.

A non-developer sales rep wired AI into his estimating software and email. Draft estimates assembled automatically. Works for any field-service business.
📒
Issue #011 · Professional services

This accountant trained her AI to close the books every month

A working CPA wired her agent into the QuickBooks API. Six weeks of training and her monthly close is now 90% one-shot — catching errors her staff missed.
📞
Issue #011 · Insurance & sales

His insurance agency texts and calls new leads in 2 minutes. Automatically.

An agent named Apollo handles every step of follow-up: instant lead response, sequenced cadence, appointment reminders, no-show recovery. He focuses on closing.
📥
Issue #010 · Time back

He let an AI triage 180 emails a day. Here's the stack.

The setup, the tone doc, the rules, and the A/B numbers from switching models. Stop losing your morning to email.
About

Hey, I'm Dhruv.

I spent 8 years in my family's wholesale business and helped grow it to 8 figures. It was an old-school industry with thin margins and no outside money, and when you're the one signing payroll checks you find out fast which ideas survive contact with a real business.

These days AI agents run a good chunk of mine. They triage my email, keep the books, find leads, and draft content. ClawDrop is where I write up the setups owners say still work after week one, so you don't have to burn your own weekends testing them.

Based in St. Louis. Every play in here comes from a real owner who's already running it.

The newsletter for people who actually run things.

Free. Tuesday mornings. Five plays. No fluff.
